title: taraxacum indigenum
intro: Taraxacum indigenum, also known as the indigenous dandelion, is a plant species in the dandelion genus that is native to certain regions and is often confused with the common dandelion due to its similar appearance. The medicinal properties of Taraxacum indigenum include its use as a diuretic and its ability to treat digestive issues, skin problems, and liver and kidney ailments. This plant is rich in vitamins and minerals, offering health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Taraxacum indigenum can be used in herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the leaves and roots in hot water or using a solvent like ethanol.
